things.name: Abritus Roman Fortress Museum
things.description: Exceptional archaeological site of the Roman fortress Abritus where Emperor Decius was killed in 251 AD; museum with coins, weapons and inscriptions

things.name: Ibrahim Pasha Mosque
things.description: The largest mosque in Bulgaria and the Balkans, built in 1616 by Ibrahim Pasha, still an active place of worship and an architectural masterpiece

things.name: Regional History Museum Razgrad
things.description: Comprehensive regional museum with exhibits on the Thracian, Roman and Ottoman eras of the Razgrad area, including treasures from Abritus

things.name: Beli Lom Nature Park
things.description: Beautiful protected nature park along the Beli Lom river with riverside walking trails, rock monasteries and rich bird life in forested gorges

things.name: Razgrad Town Square
things.description: Central square with the Town Hall, the Church of the Assumption and an archaeological garden displaying Roman finds excavated in the city centre

things.name: Art Gallery Razgrad
things.description: Municipal gallery presenting Bulgarian fine art from the 19th century to the present, with a focus on regional artists from northeast Bulgaria

things.name: Demir Baba Teke
things.description: Extraordinary Alevi Muslim shrine 30km from Razgrad set among ancient oak trees, an important pilgrimage site for Bulgarian Turks and Alevi communities

things.name: Samuil Fortress
things.description: Medieval Bulgarian fortress from the 10th century near the village of Samuil, with impressive remains of towers and walls in a scenic countryside setting

things.name: Kubrat (Day Trip)
things.description: Nearby town named after the Great Khan Kubrat, founder of the first Bulgarian state, with a museum and archaeological park on the Danube plain

things.name: Ludogorets Arena
things.description: Home of PFC Ludogorets Razgrad, Bulgaria's dominant club with 13 consecutive league titles and regular UEFA Champions and Europa League participant
